name: South Carolina Dietitian License
description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to South Carolina - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Panel for Dietetics
provider.parentOrganization: South Carolina Department of Labor, Licensing and Regulation (LLR)

renewalCycle: Every 2 years (by May 31, odd years) + 30 CE hours

requiredDocuments.name: Current Signed CDR Card
requiredDocuments.description: Copy of your signed, active Commission on Dietetic Registration credential (for licensure by registration).

requiredDocuments.name: Official College Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Sent directly to the Panel office from the degree-granting institution.

requiredDocuments.name: Valid Photo ID
requiredDocuments.description: Copy of a driver's license, state-issued ID, or passport.

requiredDocuments.name: Signed Social Security Card
requiredDocuments.description: Copy of your signed Social Security card.

requiredDocuments.name: Notarized Verification of Lawful Presence
requiredDocuments.description: Required by South Carolina law for professional licensing.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a South Carolina dietitian license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial application fee is $175 (non-refundable). The biennial renewal fee is $100, due by May 31 of odd-numbered years. Late renewal incurs a $50 fee, and reinstatement costs an additional $50.

mainEntity.name: What continuing education does South Carolina require?

acceptedAnswer.text: South Carolina requires 30 hours of continuing professional education per biennial renewal period. Additionally, maintaining CDR registration requires 75 CE hours every five years.

mainEntity.name: Does South Carolina require tele-dietetics practitioners to be licensed?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. South Carolina requires all individuals practicing dietetics or tele-dietetics within the state to hold a valid South Carolina dietitian license, regardless of where they are physically located.

mainEntity.name: Does South Carolina have reciprocity for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in South Carolina. To work in South Carolina, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to South Carolina?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in South Carolina while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.
